title: JavaScript Runtime
description: Look up every published runtime symbol and shared schema from the installed package JavaScript runtime contract on a stable static docs route.
openingSummary: This reference lists every published JavaScript runtime symbol and shared schema from the installed @you-agent-factory/api JavaScript runtime contract so dynamic-workflow authors can look up symbol paths, metadata, and shared schemas without a live Factory host.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This reference publishes the package-backed JavaScript runtime inventory for you-agent-factory. Readers can look up every published runtime symbol and shared schema from the installed JavaScript runtime contract, including available kind, visibility, mutability, nullability, examples, shared-schema links, stable anchors, and inventory filters.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: A runtime symbol path is the published JavaScript identity, such as javascript.args. The inventory comes from the installed @you-agent-factory/api/javascript/runtime public export, not from a page-local copy of symbols. Shared schemas are projected from the same package contract so authors can see reusable schema shapes the runtime publishes. Visibility, mutability, and nullability appear when the contract includes them.

runtimeInventory.title: Runtime Inventory
runtimeInventory.body: Scan the published JavaScript symbols and shared schemas below. Filter by symbol path, schema id, description, or lifecycle when those facets are present. Open a card for available metadata and shared-schema links, and copy its stable anchor when you need a deep link.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Open this page when you need the durable package JavaScript runtime lookup surface. Use the inventory filters to narrow the list, then open a symbol or shared schema for its published metadata. Prefer the authored dynamic-workflows documentation page for narrative guidance; prefer this inventory when you need every published runtime symbol and shared schema from the installed package contract.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a static package-backed JavaScript runtime inventory. It does not start a Factory host, does not invent symbol fields when the contract omits them, and is not a substitute for dynamic-workflow walkthroughs. Sibling CLI and MCP reference routes may be linked before those pages exist in every checkout.
